title: Insight Blocks Test
description: Test page demonstrating insight block types and styling

content.text: This page demonstrates all four insight block types with consistent iconography and styling.

content.title: Pro Tip: Use the Search Feature
content.content: Press / or Ctrl+K to instantly search through all pages in the Atlas. The search ranks results by title relevance, making it easy to find exactly what you need.

content.text: Tips are highlighted in yellow and use a lightbulb icon to indicate helpful advice and best practices.

content.title: Common Pitfall: Breaking Changes
content.content: When upgrading dependencies, always check the changelog for breaking changes. Test your code thoroughly before deploying to production.

content.text: Warnings are highlighted in orange and use an alert triangle icon to catch user attention.

content.title: Critical: Security Configuration
content.content: Never commit secrets or API keys to version control. Always use environment variables or secure secret management tools. This is a critical security requirement.

content.text: Important blocks are highlighted in red and use a lightning bolt icon for critical information.

content.title: Key Takeaway: Consistency is Key
content.content: The most important aspect of code design is consistency. Whether you choose tabs or spaces, camelCase or snake_case, make sure the choice is consistent throughout your project.

content.text: Takeaways are highlighted in purple and use a star icon to emphasize key points and main concepts.
Insight blocks work well alongside other content types:

content.title: Documentation Best Practice
content.content: Always include examples in your documentation. Code snippets are more valuable than lengthy explanations.

items: Write unit tests for critical paths

content.title: Testing Requirements
content.content: All new features must have corresponding unit tests. Aim for at least 80% code coverage on critical paths.
